LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT HANSON BRIDGETT, LLP'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS TO BE TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS SET ASIDE. THE COURT STATES A NEW TENTATIVE RULING TO SUSTAIN THE DEMURRER SUBJECT TO COUNSEL MEETING, CONFIRMING AND AGREEING UPON A DATE THAT THE COURT MAY RELY ON. THE COURT ALLOWS PARTIES TIME TO FILE SIMULTANEOUS SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS, 5-PAGE LIMIT, AND OR ANY STIPULATION NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 9, 2020. COURT INTENDS TO TAKE THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION ON NOVEMBER 9, 2020. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM.

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE DEC-01-2020 HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANT AMINOPTERIN, LLC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE COURT OBSERVES THAT THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS REQUIRED TO BE FILED "NO LATER THAN THE DATE FOR FILING AN OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER." (CODE CIV. PROC. SEC. 472(A).) JUDGE: ETHAN P. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compel Binding Contractual Arbitration, Staying Case Pending Result Of Arbitration Proceeding And Reserving Jurisdiction To Confirm Arbitration Award As A Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Lawrence M. Cirelli In Support
Off Calendar Aug-31-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Thomas John Lalannes, Esq. 415-434-1122 for Plaintiff.
Allan Herzlich, Esq. 818-783-8991 of Herzlich & Blum LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT HANSON BRIDGETT, LLP's Motion For Order Compel Binding Contractual Arbitration, Staying Case Pending Result Of Arbitration Proceeding And Reserving Jurisdiction To Confirm Arbitration Award As A Judgment.

Defendant Hanson Bridgett LLP's "motion for order [to] compel binding contractual arbitration, staying case pending result of arbitration proceeding and reserving jurisdiction to confirm arbitration award as a judgment" is denied without prejudice.

Code of Civil Procedure 1281.2 requires a "petition alleging the existence of a written agreement to arbitrate," and we have that allegation here. However, "[g]rounds exist for rescission of the agreement." (CCP 1281.2(b); Zebala Dec.; LaLanne Dec. Ex. A sections 5.1, 5.4.) This is not to say that the alleged arbitration agreement has been rescinded to date. Rescission of a contract requires consent by all its parties or a rescission declaration by less than all and adjudication by a court of grounds specified by law. (Civ. Code 1689.) I see no use of the word "rescind" or any derivative thereof in the parties' papers.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
